questions.stem: According to food safety guidelines, when should cafeteria staff wash their hands during food preparation?
options: Before beginning food preparation, after handling raw food, and after any interruption
Once at the start of each shift
questions.source_quote: Before beginning food preparation, after handling raw food, and after any interruption to the food-prep workflow.
questions.explanation: Proper hand hygiene requires washing before starting food preparation, after raw food contact, and after any interruption.

questions.stem: What is the recommended minimum internal temperature for cooked poultry served in a school nutrition setting?
questions.source_quote: 165°F (74°C) minimum internal temperature for poultry per USDA food safety standards.
questions.explanation: 165°F (74°C) is the USDA-recommended minimum internal temperature for poultry to ensure safe service in school nutrition programs.

questions.stem: In a school cafeteria, the temperature danger zone — where bacteria multiply most rapidly — is generally defined as the range between:
questions.source_quote: The temperature danger zone for food safety is 41°F to 135°F — the range where bacteria multiply most rapidly.
questions.explanation: The FDA Food Code defines the temperature danger zone as 41°F–135°F (5°C–57°C). Foods must not remain in this range for more than 4 cumulative hours.
